subject: physics
title: 22M.1.HL.TZ1.24
question_html:
P and Q are two moons of equal densities orbiting a planet. The orbital radius of P is twice the orbital radius of Q. The volume of P is half that of Q. The force exerted by the planet on P is F. What is the force exerted by the planet on Q?

A.  F

B.  2F

C.  4F

D.  8F
examiners_html: Option D was the most frequent (correct) answer, however option C was a significant distractor, perhaps for candidates considering only the change in orbital radius. A relatively high discrimination index was seen with this question.
